candidates.why: Exact total current assets line on the balance sheet; directly matches ACT.
Current asset component only, not the total.
Non-current assets, not current assets.

candidates.row_label: Gains from divestments of non-current assets
candidates.why: Cash flow item unrelated to balance sheet current assets.
Exact balance sheet cash line; directly matches CHE.

candidates.row_label: Cash and cash equivalents at the end of the period
candidates.why: Cash flow reconciliation line, but CHE should come from balance sheet when available.

candidates.row_label: Cash and cash equivalents at the beginning of the period
candidates.why: Beginning balance, not period-end balance sheet cash.

candidates.row_label: Effect of exchange rate changes on cash
candidates.why: Cash flow adjustment, not cash balance.

candidates.row_label: Investments in subsidiaries (net of acquired cash)
candidates.why: Investing cash flow item, not cash balance.

candidates.row_label: Share redemption TT (cash and cash equivalents)
candidates.why: Transaction line, not cash balance.

variables.notes: Balance sheet cash label is explicit and matches the cash flow ending cash.

candidates.why: Exact total current liabilities line on the balance sheet; directly matches LCT.
Component of current liabilities only, not the total.
Cash flow change line, not the balance sheet stock variable.
Non-current liabilities, not current liabilities.
Balancing line, not a reported total current liabilities figure.
Non-current liabilities total, not current liabilities.

candidates.row_label: Change in non-current liabilities and related derivatives
candidates.why: Cash flow change line, not current liabilities stock.
This is the current portion of long-term debt and matches the STD definition. It excludes leases and is cleaner than lease-related rows.

Semantically could be borrowings/bank debt; one occurrence appears in the current-liabilities section and another in non-current liabilities. Because the same label appears in multiple places, using it would risk ambiguity from row-label-only mapping.
Cash flow movement, not the balance sheet short-term debt stock.

variables.notes: R:c. Selected the explicit current portion of debt. Excluded all lease-liability rows per instruction. Although a current 'Liabilities to Credit Institutions' line may exist, the identical label also appears for non-current debt, so the explicit maturities-within-1-year label is the safer exact mapping.

candidates.why: Exact current tax payable/current tax liabilities line; directly matches TXP.
Balance sheet PPE-type asset line and appears to function as the main reported tangible fixed asset label in the latest year.
Net tangible fixed assets component; likely used in earlier years when the company split PPE more granularly.
Another PPE component in earlier years; may need to be combined with equipment when PPE is split.
Tangible-like long-term asset but lease-related and usually not the preferred baseline PPE line for accruals.
Intangibles, not PPE.
Intangibles, not PPE.
Capex flow, not balance sheet PPE stock.

candidates.row_label: Depreciation and amortization of tangible and intangible assets
candidates.why: Expense flow, not PPE stock.

candidates.row_label: Divestments of tangible and intangible assets
candidates.why: Disposal cash flow, not PPE stock.
Intangible capex, not PPE stock.

variables.notes: R:c. No explicit total PPE/PP&E line is shown. Latest year appears to use 'Equipment', while earlier years appear split across 'Equipment and vehicles, Net' and 'Buildings and land Net'. Manual review is warranted because the firm’s PPE presentation changes over time and the selected labels may need year-specific treatment or summation.

candidates.why: Exact balance sheet total assets line; directly matches AT.
Exact net cash flow from operating activities line; directly matches OANCF.

candidates.row_label: Cash flow from operating activities before changes inworking capital
candidates.why: Pre-working-capital subtotal, not the final net operating cash flow.

variables.notes: Selected the final net operating cash flow line rather than the pre-working-capital subtotal.
